kind: algorithmic complexity-class replacement (O(n log n) -> O(n)) for descending sort/argsort
lever_summary: Descending sort/argsort was gated out of the radix paths (always generic comparison sort). Descending == ascending radix of the complement key (key ^ u64::MAX); stable radix keeps equal keys in ascending index order = generic stable descending. A key_mask XORed into the radix key drives it across i64/f64 dense + f32/f16/bf16/u32/u64/i32 literal paths.
method: same-bench A/B; baseline = stashed generic descending sort; normalized by eval/sort_calib_reduce_64k_i64 (ReduceSum, unaffected) — workers measured identical.

change.lever: Added descending param + key_mask (u64::MAX when descending) to sort_along_axis_dense_i64/_f64/_literal_radix; sort_along_axis routes descending through them. Added eval/sort_64k_f64_descending bench + radix_sort_descending_matches_generic test.

isomorphism.type: BIT-IDENTICAL permutation to generic stable descending: !key ascending == key descending (exact reversal), stable radix keeps equal keys ascending-index == stable sort_by(compare_sort_keys(b,a))
isomorphism.validation: radix_sort_descending_matches_generic (f64/i64 dense-vs-Literal + f32 vs reference) + pre-existing sort_1d_descending + 16 sort tests pass

validation.name: sort_tests
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --release sort

validation.name: clippy
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --release --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings

note: Completes radix coverage of sort/argsort: ascending (pass98/99) + descending (pass102), all single-key numeric dtypes; Complex stays generic (2-key).
